description: Implement three new modules: Real-Time Thermal Simulation Engine, 3D Cable Routing & Containment System, and Intelligent Energy Monitoring Dashboard. Each module gets a navigation tab, full trilingual support, state management, calculation engine, SVG visualization, and comprehensive tests.
steps: STEP 1 - Register three new module keys in the translation objects (around lines 625-970 of el-dimensionering.html): Add 'thermal', 'cable3d', 'energy' to T.da.modules, T.en.modules, T.fa.modules. Danish: 'Termisk Sim.', '3D Kabelfoering', 'Energi Monitor'. English: 'Thermal Sim', '3D Cable Routing', 'Energy Monitor'. Farsi: appropriate translations. Also add entries to _FA dictionary for tx() strings used by the modules.

STEP 2 - Add three cases to the renderModule() switch statement (around line 2720): case 'thermal': content = renderThermal(); break; case 'cable3d': content = renderCable3D(); break; case 'energy': content = renderEnergy(); break;

STEP 3 - Implement the Thermal Simulation Engine module (insert before the INIT section near end of file). This module needs: (a) THERMAL_CABLE_DATA constant with temperature coefficients per cable insulation type (PVC: 70C max, XLPE: 90C max per DS/HD 60364-5-52 Table B.52.14/15), ambient derating factors, grouping factors. (b) thermalState object with fields: ambientTemp (default 25), loadFactor (default 0.8), selectedCircuit (null), simRunning (false), simTime (0), groupingCount (1). (c) thermalCalcTemp(cableType, In, Iz, ambientC, groupFactor) function returning current temp, max temp, margin. (d) thermalCalcI2t(In, tripTime) function for I-squared-t energy. (e) thermalCalcDerating(ambientC, groupCount, installMethod) per Table B.52.14/15. (f) thermalGetColor(tempC, maxC) returning color gradient blue->green->yellow->red->danger. (g) renderThermal() function returning HTML with: ambient temperature slider (20-45C range input), load factor slider (0.1-1.5), grouping count buttons (1-9), cable type selector buttons (PVC/XLPE), installation method buttons, SVG animated visualization showing cables with color-gradient fill based on temperature, I2t race condition bar chart, result cards with clause references. ALL inputs must be sliders or buttons - NO text inputs.

STEP 4 - Implement the 3D Cable Routing & Containment module. Needs: (a) CABLE3D_CONTAINMENT constant with tray types (perforated, wire mesh, solid, conduit) with fill rates per DS/HD 60364-5-52 Table 52.3 (max 45% for conduit). (b) CABLE3D_BEND_RADIUS constant with minimum radii per cable type (XLPE: 8x diameter, PVC: 6x diameter per IEC 60228). (c) cable3dState object with: selectedTray (null), viewAngle (30), cables array, selectedSegment (null). (d) cable3dCalcFillRate(trayType, cables) function. (e) cable3dCalcGroupDerating(cableCount, trayType) per Table B.52.17. (f) cable3dCheckBendRadius(cableType, diameter) function. (g) cable3dCalcHeatDissipation(cables) function. (h) renderCable3D() function returning HTML with: isometric 3D SVG view of cable trays rendered with transforms, cable bundles shown as colored circles inside containment cross-section, tray type selector buttons, cable add/remove buttons, view rotation slider, fill-rate gauge, bend radius violation indicators, grouping derating results. Click tray segments to see details. All click-only UI.

STEP 5 - Implement the Energy Monitoring Dashboard module. Needs: (a) ENERGY_TARIFFS constant with current Danish tariff structure (nettarif, systemtarif, elafgift, PSO - use 2024 rates from Energinet: ~0.11 DKK/kWh nettarif, ~0.06 systemtarif, ~0.008 elafgift, total transport ~0.30-0.90 DKK/kWh depending on time). (b) ENERGY_LOAD_PROFILES constant with typical 24h profiles for residential/office/industrial (array of 24 hourly multipliers). (c) ENERGY_HARMONIC_SOURCES constant with typical THD% for common non-linear loads (LED: 15-25%, VFD: 30-40%, PC: 80-120%, EV charger: 5-12%). (d) energyState object with: profileType ('residential'), hour (12), capacitorKvar (0), selectedLoad (null), simRunning (false). (e) energyCalcPowerFactor(loads) function. (f) energyCalcCapacitorSize(currentPF, targetPF, kW) per DS/HD 60364-5-52 cl.523. (g) energyCalcTHD(loads) function computing total harmonic distortion. (h) energyCalcDailyCost(profile, tariffs, peakKW) function. (i) energyCalcPeakShaving(profile) returning recommendations. (j) energyGetPQStatus(voltage, frequency, thd, flicker, unbalance) returning traffic light status. (k) renderEnergy() function returning HTML with: building type selector buttons, 24h load profile SVG chart with hour slider, power factor gauge with capacitor sizing buttons, harmonic spectrum bar chart SVG, cost breakdown card with Danish tariffs, peak shaving recommendation card, PQ traffic light indicators (voltage, frequency, THD, flicker, unbalance). All click-only.

STEP 6 - Add comprehensive tests to test-sld.js. Add three new test sections: === Thermal Simulation Tests ===, === 3D Cable Routing Tests ===, === Energy Monitoring Tests ===. Aim for 15-17 tests per module (45-51 total new tests). Tests must cover: (a) Function existence checks, (b) Calculation correctness with known inputs/outputs, (c) DS/HD 60364 clause compliance (conservative values), (d) Edge cases (0 load, max temp, 100% fill rate), (e) HTML output verification (no text inputs, correct structure), (f) State management correctness. Update the final summary count.

STEP 7 - Verify all tests pass by ensuring consistent function names between implementation and tests. Ensure the renderModule switch handles all three new cases. Ensure _FA translations cover all English strings used in the modules.
acceptance_criteria: Running 'node test-sld.js' passes all tests (110 existing + 45-51 new = 155-161 total)
Three new navigation tabs appear in all three languages
renderModule() correctly dispatches to all three new render functions
Thermal simulation calculates cable temperature with derating per DS/HD 60364-5-52 Table B.52.14/15
Cable 3D module enforces 45% max fill rate for conduit per Table 52.3
Energy module uses real Danish tariff structure with correct cost calculation
All three modules return HTML with zero <input type='text'> or <textarea> elements
All calculations cite specific DS/HD 60364 or IEC clauses
Conservative calculations throughout (never underestimate temperature, never overestimate capacity)
verification: Run 'node test-sld.js' and confirm output shows 155+ passed, 0 failed
Grep for '<input type="text"' in the new render functions to confirm zero matches
Verify each new module appears in T.da.modules, T.en.modules, and T.fa.modules
findings: All 160 tests pass (110 existing + 50 new). Thermal module uses thermalState.loadFactor internally so tests must account for this. Color thresholds: blue <=0.5, green <=0.7, yellow <=0.85, red <=1.0, darkred >1.0 of max temp. Review fixes applied: (1) THERMAL_AMPACITY_TABLE added with proper Iz lookup per cross-section/method/type, (2) thermalCalcDerating now accepts optional cableType param, (3) PQ section labeled illustrative, (4) cable3dCalcGroupDerating extrapolates beyond 9 with warning, (5) CABLE3D_GROUP_DERATING consolidated as reference to THERMAL_GROUP_FACTORS.
